title: Mowing quality and turf protection
summary: Match mowing height and frequency to turf condition, avoid removing more than one-third of the leaf blade, and leave an even finish without scalping or clumps.
detail: Inspect moisture, height, obstacles, slopes, and mower condition before starting. Change direction periodically and stop if soil is saturated enough to rut.
preparationSteps: Walk the area and remove debris
Confirm discharge direction will not endanger people or property
prohibitedActions: Discharging debris toward people, vehicles, or windows
environmentalConcerns: Keep clippings out of storm drains
laborConsiderations: Wet or overgrown turf increases time

title: Pacific Northwest wet-turf operations
summary: In prolonged wet weather, protect soil structure and turf by postponing work that would rut, compact, smear, or tear saturated ground.
detail: Portland-area winter and shoulder-season conditions can keep soil saturated. Calendar timing alone is not enough; inspect actual site conditions.
requiredObservations: forecast if live data is available
procedureSteps: Notify the customer when service changes materially
commonMistakes: Assuming a scheduled date means conditions are suitable
prohibitedActions: Inventing current weather or soil moisture
safetyWarnings: Wet slopes increase slip and equipment rollover risk
laborConsiderations: Hand work may be slower than mechanized work

title: Irrigation leak triage
summary: Isolate the affected zone, document the leak location and behavior, prevent avoidable water loss, and diagnose only from observed evidence.
detail: Separate emitter, lateral, valve, mainline, controller, and drainage symptoms. Do not claim a buried component failed without testing.
preparationSteps: Confirm authorization before operating customer controls
procedureSteps: Stop active water loss when safe
Test one zone at a time
commonMistakes: Replacing parts before isolating the fault
safetyWarnings: Water near electrical equipment requires immediate shutdown and escalation
licensingConcerns: Verify Oregon or Washington requirements before work involving regulated plumbing, backflow, or electrical systems
laborConsiderations: Buried fault location and access are unknown until inspected

title: Standing-water assessment
summary: Treat standing water as a symptom requiring site observations, not as proof of one cause.
detail: Possible causes include saturated soil, compaction, grading, blocked conveyance, irrigation leakage, roof discharge, or a high seasonal water table.
preparationSteps: Determine whether a building or electrical hazard exists
procedureSteps: Separate immediate mitigation from permanent design
commonMistakes: Recommending a drain without elevations or outlet feasibility
prohibitedActions: Guaranteeing a drainage solution without site design information
safetyWarnings: Escalate water near foundations, electrical systems, or unstable slopes
environmentalConcerns: Do not direct runoff onto neighboring property or polluted drains
licensingConcerns: Drainage design, excavation, plumbing, or permitted work may require licensed professionals
laborConsiderations: Investigation and design time are separate from installation

title: Mulch depth and placement
summary: Apply an appropriate even mulch layer while keeping material away from trunks, crowns, siding, and drainage paths.
detail: Measure bed area and existing mulch depth before calculating material. Required cubic yards equal square feet times depth in feet divided by 27.
preparationSteps: Remove prohibited debris and weeds as scoped
procedureSteps: Pull mulch back from trunks and crowns
prohibitedActions: Burying root flare or plant crowns
safetyWarnings: Use safe lifting and wheelbarrow practices
environmentalConcerns: Prevent material from entering storm drains
laborConsiderations: Haul distance, stairs, slope, and staging materially affect labor

title: Uncertain plant identification
summary: Do not state a definitive plant identification without sufficient observable features.
detail: Useful observations include whole-plant habit, leaf arrangement, leaf detail, stem or bark, flowers or fruit, scale, season, and location.
ppe: gloves when plant hazards are unknown
preparationSteps: Avoid contact with unknown sap, spines, or fruit
procedureSteps: Offer ranked possibilities only when supported
Request missing photos or specialist review
safetyWarnings: Treat unknown plants as potentially irritating or toxic
environmentalConcerns: Do not remove a suspected invasive until identification and disposal requirements are verified
laborConsiderations: Additional inspection or specialist time may be required

title: Tree-risk observation and escalation
summary: Field staff may document visible warning signs but must not represent a limited observation as a formal tree-risk assessment.
detail: Warning signs can include recent root plate movement, hanging or cracked limbs, major cavities, active failure, or contact with energized conductors.
preparationSteps: Keep people out of the suspected drop zone
procedureSteps: Stop work in the hazard area
Document without entering the drop zone
Refer to a qualified arborist or utility
qualityStandard: Hazard area controlled and escalation documented
commonMistakes: Standing under a suspect limb for a photo
prohibitedActions: Declaring a tree safe without qualified assessment
safetyWarnings: Call emergency services for immediate life safety hazards
licensingConcerns: Tree work and formal assessment may require qualified or licensed specialists
laborConsiderations: Specialist assessment and traffic or site control are separate scope

title: Licensing and professional boundaries
summary: Do not claim a task is legally permitted for Urban Yards unless current jurisdiction, scope, and credentials have been verified.
detail: Requirements can change and differ between Oregon, Washington, Portland, Vancouver, and project types. Use current authoritative sources and escalate uncertain regulated work.
preparationSteps: Verify current requirements from an authoritative source
qualityStandard: No unsupported legal or licensing claim
prohibitedActions: Inventing a license, certification, or permit status
safetyWarnings: Stop work when scope exceeds training or authorization
licensingConcerns: Always verify current law for regulated scope
laborConsiderations: Specialist review and permits affect schedule and cost

title: Urban Yards estimating assumptions
summary: Every estimate must show supplied measurements, calculation assumptions, labor basis, access constraints, disposal, travel, overhead, contingency, and margin where applicable.
detail: Never invent dimensions or production rates. Use approved property measurements, documented site observations, or clearly labeled user-supplied assumptions.
procedureSteps: Apply approved overhead, contingency, and margin
commonMistakes: Confusing labor hours with crew hours
prohibitedActions: Presenting a draft as a final contractual price
laborConsiderations: Production rates must be sourced or approved

title: Urban Yards field documentation
summary: Document arrival condition, completed work, exceptions, hazards, and recommended follow-up in the ticket record.
detail: Photos and notes are evidence, not permission to save uncertain diagnoses as permanent property facts.
preparationSteps: Review property instructions and ticket scope
procedureSteps: Request clarification when scope is uncertain
qualityStandard: Record is sufficient for owner review and customer communication
prohibitedActions: Exposing confidential financial data to unauthorized field roles
laborConsiderations: Documentation time belongs in job planning

title: Urban Yards service quality closeout
summary: Before closeout, compare completed work to scope, verify cleanup, capture required proof, record exceptions, and identify unapproved recommendations separately.
detail: A recommendation is not completed work. Missing requirements must be completed or explicitly marked N/A with a reason.
qualityStandard: Every requirement complete or explicitly N/A
environmentalConcerns: Confirm drains and public areas are clean
laborConsiderations: Rework and documentation should be tracked
